name: step-by-step
description: /step-by-step (alias /sbs) — plan-first sequential runbook with FOUR execution modes on a question-density spectrum (fewest → most questions: go ── auto ── manual ── full). Always decomposes into an ordered numbered plan (except GO, which suppresses it); modes differ in question density + what happens between steps. GO (0.3.0, fewest Q) — no plan gate, execute end-to-end terse, result lines only; fastest/most-trusting for unambiguous tasks. AUTO — show plan, execute every step strictly in order WITHOUT pausing (the pre-0.2.0 single-shot behavior). MANUAL (the DEFAULT, 0.2.0) — execute ONE step at a time, then pause (`⏸ step i/N done. next → … proceed?`) and hand control back, ending the turn; next step runs only on the user's go-ahead (consult/steer at every boundary). FULL (0.3.0, most Q) — disambiguate FIRST: before planning, run AskUserQuestion rounds (≤4/round) round-after-round until a scan finds ZERO remaining ambiguity ('묻고 또 묻고'), state `🔍 disambiguation: N rounds · ambiguity → 0`, then execute straight-through BUT stop + ask another round whenever a step surfaces NEW ambiguity (never guess past a fork). FULL never assumes — every unknown is a question (the high-Q end for underspecified / high-stakes work); the other 3 modes assume + proceed (state the assumption). Mode = FIRST arg token `go` | `auto` | `manual` | `full` (default manual); remainder is the task. ALL modes halt on a step failure (report failing step + verbatim error + un-run tail) and pause before a genuinely irreversible / destructive / outward-facing step (confirm-then-resume, same bar as the bypass self-check). The deliberate single-threaded counterpart to /cycle's parallel fan-out — one thread, top to bottom. NL triggers: /step-by-step · /sbs · go/auto/manual/full 모드 토큰.
